statement.markdown: Write a function to validate Docker container port mappings. A valid port mapping is defined as follows: if the port number in the host machine is greater than or equal to 1024 (non-standard port) and the port number in the container is greater than or equal to 1 (a valid port number in a container).
statement.inputFormat: A string representing a Docker container port mapping in the format 'hostPort:containerPort'.
statement.outputFormat: True if the port mapping is valid, False otherwise.
statement.notes: Assume that the input string is well-formed and does not contain any invalid characters.

examples.explanation: The host port is non-standard and the container port is valid.
The host port is a standard port and the container port is valid.

hints.text: Check if the host port is non-standard
Check if the container port is valid

editorial.title: DOCKER Container Port Mapping Validator
editorial.intuition: Think about the definition of a valid port mapping.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a single function to check both conditions.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a single line of code to check both conditions.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Prove that the solution checks both conditions.
editorial.dryRun: Test the solution with different input values.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to check one of the conditions.
